message.abstract:
<jats:p>Understanding the mortality impact of COVID-19 requires not only counting the dead, but analyzing how premature the deaths are. We calculate years of life lost (YLL) across 42 countries due to COVID-19 attributable deaths, and also conduct an analysis based on estimated excess deaths. As of June 13th 2020, YLL in heavily affected countries are 2 to 6 times the average seasonal influenza; over two thirds of the YLL result from deaths in ages below 75 and one quarter from deaths below 55; and men have lost 47% more life years than women. The results confirm the large mortality impact of COVID-19 among the elderly. They also call for heightened awareness in devising policies that protect vulnerable demographics losing the largest number of life-years.</jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>One Sentence Summary</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Across 42 countries, the years of life lost due to COVID-19 are up to 6 times that of the average seasonal flu.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Global years of life lost to COVID-19
